content.problemStatement:
In Chefland, each person has their own non-empty personal string. When two people with personal strings T1T_1T1​ and T2T_2T2​ (possibly identical) become friends in Chefland, their strings are replaced by a new string S=T1+T1+T2+T2S = T_1 + T_1 + T_2 + T_2S=T1​+T1​+T2​+T2​, where +++ denotes string concatenation.

Chef recently became friends with Chefina. However, Chefina's personal string was her favourite and she got upset when it was replaced by SSS. She wanted her personal string back. Chef does not remember T1T_1T1​ and T2T_2T2​ now, he only knows SSS.

Find the number of ways in which Chef can retrieve valid strings T1T_1T1​ and T2T_2T2​ from the given string SSS. It is also possible that Chef does not remember SSS correctly, in which case there is no way to retrieve T1T_1T1​ and T2T_2T2​.
constraints: SSS contains only lowercase English letters
the sum of ∣S∣|S|∣S∣ over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1062 \cdot 10^62⋅106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single string SSS.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the number of ways to retrieve T1T_1T1​ and T2T_2T2​ from SSS.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: There is no way to choose T1T_1T1​ and T2T_2T2​.
Example case 2: Both T1T_1T1​ and T2T_2T2​ must be "a".
